bindings.quest_action: anonymously send defensive supplies to the settlement I abandoned
bindings.quest_focus: the settlement I fled during a raid years ago
bindings.quest_stakes: the same threat that drove me away is closing in on them again
bindings.quest_threat: the shame of having abandoned people who counted on me
bindings.quest_origin: years ago I ran from a raid and left everyone behind

dialogueChunks.intro: You look like someone who's seen the world, {player_name}. Tell me: do you believe a person can outrun their past?
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {quest_origin}. Friends, neighbors, people who counted on me. Now {quest_stakes}: {location} is facing the same {enemy_type} threat, and {target_npc} is there. I want to {quest_action}, gathered from {location_hint}. I can't face them yet. But I can stop being useless.
dialogueChunks.outro: I know this doesn't erase what I did. But {quest_threat} doesn't go away by doing nothing a second time.

playerResponses.text: Trying to make it right takes courage too. I'll help.
Anonymous help? Seems like you're still running. But I'll gather the supplies.
Your guilt isn't my burden. Deal with it yourself.

bindings.quest_action: restock the settlement's medicine with pure, undiluted remedies
bindings.quest_focus: the medicine I diluted for profit that cost people their lives
bindings.quest_stakes: two or three people died because the remedies I made were too weak
bindings.quest_threat: the healer who couldn't understand why treatments kept failing
bindings.quest_origin: greed that led me to water down medicine during a plague

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, I've spent a long time on the road trying to forget something I did. The road hasn't helped.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: When I worked at the tavern in {location}, {quest_origin}. {quest_stakes}. {target_npc} was {quest_threat}. I need to make amends. There's a source of pure {quest_item} {location_hint} that could {quest_action}. Real supplies, this time. No shortcuts.
dialogueChunks.outro: I can't bring back the dead. But {quest_focus} doesn't have to claim anyone else.

playerResponses.text: What you did was terrible. But wanting to fix it is real. I'll help.
People died because of your greed. Getting supplies doesn't even the score.
Turn yourself in. That's the only real redemption.

bindings.quest_action: rebuild a destroyed memorial site to honor a child I failed to save
bindings.quest_focus: the night I froze while a child died in a burning building
bindings.quest_stakes: the father still greets me kindly, not knowing I could have saved his daughter
bindings.quest_threat: a guilt that cuts deeper every time the father smiles at me
bindings.quest_origin: a moment of cowardice during an attack that cost a child's life

dialogueChunks.intro: Sit with me for a moment, {player_name}. I have a story I've never told anyone, and it's killing me to keep it inside.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: I used to be a guard in {location}. One night during a {enemy_type} attack, {quest_focus}. I had the chance to help but I froze. {target_npc} was the father. {quest_stakes}, and {quest_threat} is unbearable. There's a memorial site {location_hint} destroyed by {enemy_type}. If I {quest_action} with proper {quest_item}, maybe I can start to live with myself.
dialogueChunks.outro: I don't expect forgiveness. {quest_origin} haunts me, but I can't carry this alone anymore.

playerResponses.text: Rebuilding won't undo the past, but it honors the memory. I'll help.
That's a heavy burden. I'll gather what you need.
You should tell {target_npc} the truth. Not build monuments to avoid it.
